title: Bridge Essay: Voice-selective prediction alterations in nonclinical voice hearers.
concept: Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.

body.text: This bridge paper connects **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.

**Voice-selective prediction alterations in nonclinical voice hearers.** — Sci Rep (2018). DOI: 10.1038/s41598-018-32614-9. PMID: 30283058. Auditory verbal hallucinations (AVH) are a cardinal symptom of psychosis but also occur in 6-13% of the general population. Voice perception is thought to engage an internal forward model that generates predictions, preparing the auditory cortex for upcoming sensory feedback. Impaired processing of sensory feedback in vocalization seems to underlie the experience of AVH in psychosis, but whether this is the case in nonclinical voice hearers remains unclear.
